Title: Subcellular Distribution of Glutathione Precursors in Arabidopsis thaliana

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the ATP-dependent ligation of glutamate and cysteine to form γ-glutamylcysteine, the first intermediate of glutathione biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Important antioxidant with roles in plant development, growth and defense.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Protoplast compartments including plastids, cytosol, nuclei, mitochondria and peroxisomes; absent from apoplast in this study
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastids are considered the main production center for γ-EC in Arabidopsis; glutathione-synthesizing organelles discussed as plastids and cytosol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Impairment or inhibition of GSH1 strongly decreases γ-EC and glutathione and causes accumulation of cysteine, glutamate and glycine in most cell compartments; complementation with GSH1 restores glutathione synthesis and precursor distribution.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key control/committed first step of glutathione synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pad2-1 point mutant analysis; chemical inhibition with buthionine sulfoximine (BSO); genetic complementation/overexpression in OE3; immunogold labeling and subcellular metabolite quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 2 mM buthionine sulfoximine for 48 h inhibits GSH1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSH1 encodes the first enzyme of glutathione synthesis in Arabidopsis. 2. A pad2-1 point mutation in GSH1 or BSO inhibition caused strong depletion of γ-EC in all compartments and major reduction of glutathione. 3. Under GSH1 impairment, cysteine, glutamate and glycine accumulated in most compartments including plastids and cytosol. 4. Complementation of pad2-1 with Arabidopsis GSH1 cDNA (OE3) restored glutathione synthesis and largely normalized precursor levels.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating GSH1 is an effective route to modulate glutathione biosynthesis and cellular redox capacity, but reduced GSH1 activity strongly lowers glutathione and perturbs precursor homeostasis; overexpression/complementation can restore glutathione-deficient phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced GSH1 activity in pad2-1 causes severe glutathione deficiency and broad shifts in precursor pools across compartments.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: pad2-1 carries a G-to-A transition at position 1697 from the start codon, causing Ser298Asn substitution in the 522-aa GSH1 protein. OE3 is a complemented transgenic line overexpressing Arabidopsis GSH1 cDNA in the pad2-1 background.
